caption: Functional localizer for brain regions with differential activity to deontic (yellow) and utilitarian (green) processing (p < 0.005, extent ≥ 53). Regions were classified as deontic when the participant indicated that their choice was based on rights and wrongs, and utilitarian when their choice was based on costs and benefits. These regions were then applied to the passive phase activation in which each statement had been presented individually in the absence of choice. Each statement was categorized based on whether the participant sold a particular personal value during an auction held after the brain imaging session (bid) or opted out of the auction for that value (optout). At the time of imaging, participants did not know that they were going to have the opportunity to sell these values for real money. The left temporoparietal junction (MNI coordinates: −63, −39, 42) showed significantly greater activity for the optout statements than the bid statements (T = 3.19, p = 0.003), indicating that these were processed in the deontic region. Both the left and right inferior parietal lobule (MNI coordinates: −45, −72, 46 and 48, −66, 35) had the opposite pattern (T = 4.09, p = 0.001), which was driven primarily by the not chosen bid statements, indicating that these statements were processed in utilitarian regions. Vertical scale on bar graphs is estimated beta values for the individual conditions ± s.e.m. across all subjects.
answers: A1: The imaging modality is not specifically mentioned in the caption.
A2: The image depicts the brain, specifically the left temporoparietal junction and both the left and right inferior parietal lobule.
A3: The caption does not mention any abnormalities in these brain regions.
A4: The image appears normal as per the caption; it only shows differential processing in specific brain regions based on deontic and utilitarian decision-making.

A5: Yes, the image contains a significant label or index that identifies different brain regions involved in deontic (yellow) and utilitarian (green) processing. Notably, the left temporoparietal junction and both left and right inferior parietal lobule show a specific pattern of activity during deontic and utilitarian decision-making, respectively. Also, MNI coordinates are provided for these regions.

caption: Cranial surgery in cow (a–c) compared with two human crania from the Neolithic period in France ((d) [28217bis], (e) [17144]). The cranial surgery in the cow cranium does not appear different to cranial surgery practiced on human crania. The use of a low magnification approach with either hand lenses or binoculars is more practical for identifying complete assemblages of cut-marks than a scanning electronic microscope27. More of the cut-marks appear in groups crossing between them and are obliquely orientated to the border of the perforation (white arrows). Parallel long cut-marks produced by a single tool in a unique gesture can be seen in cow as in human crania (black arrows). Other cut-marks with similar orientation show a large space between them and are almost parallel to the border of perforation; these cut-marks are probably associated with cutting more than with grasping (chevrons). Bar corresponds to 1 cm.
answers: A1: The imaging modality is not specified in the caption, but it discusses observations made from a physical examination, potentially with the aid of hand lenses or binoculars.

A3: The abnormalities identified are not health problems but are rather cut-marks indicating cranial surgery or human intervention.

A4: The image does not show any irregularities related to natural anatomy, but does display evidence of cranial surgery, which is not a normal characteristic found in humans and animals without human intervention.

A5: Yes, this image contains several labels or indices that are significant. caption: Microscopic appearance.A loupe view of the resected Meckel’s diverticulum shows almost normal ileal wall with well-formed villi and the muscularis propria (right side of upper picture). The tip revealed a mucosal herniation (arrow) through the diverticular wall, outpouching in the mesentery. This outpouching area showed poorly-developed muscularis mucosa and lacked the muscularis propria (left side of upper picture). The mucosa shows erosions, villous atrophy, and lymphoid hyperplasia, surrounded with marked fibroelastosis. Lower pictures stained with hematoxylin & eosin (HE, left) and Elastica van Gieson (EVG, right) are higher magnifications of the square area of upper picture.
answers: A1: The imaging modality is not explicitly stated in the caption, but it refers to microscopic examination of a tissue sample. This suggests that the image probably represents histopathology.
A2: The image depicts a resected Meckel’s diverticulum, an abnormal tissue found in the ileal wall (part of the small intestine), and the surrounding mesentery.

A3: Yes, there are several abnormalities identified. The tip of the Meckel’s diverticulum revealed a mucosal herniation through the diverticular wall, outpouching in the mesentery. This outpouched area lacks proper development of muscularis mucosa and lack the muscularis propria. Additionally, the mucosa has erosions, villous atrophy (shrinking), and lymphoid hyperplasia (abnormal increase in the number of normal cells), with marked fibroelastosis (abnormal development of fibrous tissue and elastic fibers).
A4: The image does not appear normal due to the described irregularities and abnormalities.

A5: Yes, the left side of the upper picture and the lower pictures contain noteworthy labels. The left side of the upper picture denotes a section that lacks muscular propria with a herniated mucosal tissue. The lower pictures are stained with hematoxylin & eosin (HE) and Elastica van Gieson (EVG), known for highlighting different structures in the tissue.

caption: Histopathological changes of skeletal muscles in DMD patients. Muscle biopsies taken from the patients on day 0, before cellular therapy, revealed the predominance of fat or connective tissue; these changes corresponded to Grade 4 according to the Muntoni scale [20]. In the biopsy taken from Patient 1 on day 0, focal muscle fibres were surrounded by fat and connective tissue. However, during the follow-up period after six months, more numerous muscle fibres in the cell-grafted area were present. In the biopsy from Patient 2, on day 0, abundant substitutions by fat and connective tissue were evident. Four weeks after cellular therapy, focal myofibres were detected at the cell-grafted site, however, after six months, only the fibrotic and fat tissues were detected in the examined biopsies. Biopsy taken from patient 3 also revealed only fat and connective tissue at day 0. Four weeks after the cell transplantation, muscle tissue with disrupted muscle fascicles, marked fibrotic tissue and abundant inflammatory infiltrates was visible. Six months after cellular therapy, inflammatory infiltrates between the muscle fibres were very moderate compared to week 4.
answers: A1: The caption does not provide information on the imaging modality used.

A4: The image shows irregularities such as the predominance of fat or connective tissue, disrupted muscle fascicles, marked fibrotic tissue, and inflammatory infiltrates.
A5: Yes, the image caption refers to the Muntoni scale to grade the histopathological changes, indicating severity of the condition. The mention of grade 4 indicates a severe stage.

caption: A visual exploration of physician‐corrected segmentation pairs with the least interobserver agreement. Case LUNG1‐005 accounts for the worst Dice similarity coefficient (DSC), surface DSC, and κ values between any pair of pleural effusion segmentations. RO1 mistook atelectatic lung for effusion, but Rad3 and Rad4 did not. Case LUNG1‐253 accounts for the worst 95HD and symmetric ASD values between any pair of pleural effusion segmentations. Rad3 and Rad4 varied in how much trace pleural fluid they chose to segment. This exposes a weakness in our pleural effusion segmentation methodology because we did not decide at projection initiation whether or to what extent trace pleural fluid should be part of the segmentation. Case LUNG1‐026 accounts for the worst sDSC value between any pair of thoracic cavity segmentations. RO3 failed to segment the full extent of peri‐mediastinal primary gross tumor volume, right effusion, and left hilum (orange arrows). Case LUNG1‐354 accounts for the worst DSC, κ, 95HD, and symmetric ASD values between any pair of thoracic cavity segmentations. RO3 erroneously excluded a collapsed left lung. [Color figure can be viewed at wileyonlinelibrary.com]
answers: A1: The caption does not provide information on the specific imaging modality represented in the image.
A2: The anatomical areas referred to in the caption include the thoracic cavity, lung, and pleural effusion, indicating that the images depict the chest area.
A3: Yes, there are abnormalities identified in the image. These include pleural effusion, peri-mediastinal primary gross tumor volume, right effusion, a collapsed left lung, and trace pleural fluid.

A4: The image shows irregularities, including incorrect segmentation of pleural effusion and a failure in segmenting thoracic cavity elements such as a peri-mediastinal primary gross tumor volume and the full extent of the left hilum. Also, a case of a collapsed left lung is mentioned.

A5: Yes, the image contains significant indices such as Dice similarity coefficient (DSC), surface DSC, κ values, 95HD, symmetric ASD, and sDSC values, which are used to measure the accuracy of the image segmentation. The specific cases LUNG1-005, LUNG1-253, LUNG1-026, and LUNG1-354 show the worst values of these metrics compared to any other pair of respective segmentations.

caption: Phagocytosis of a laser-irradiated astrocyte within a cell cluster.An astrocyte within a five-cell cluster is lysed via laser nanoablation along the yellow ROI, visible in the first two images. Contrast enhanced, higher magnification images of the irradiated region are displayed in the second and fourth rows labeled “High-mag”. High magnification images correspond to the region within the outlined box in both pre and post laser-irradiated cell images. Immediately following laser exposure, the targeted cell shows visible signs of death including membrane retraction from the dish surface, phase paling of the nucleus, and phase dark material aggregating around the laser-irradiated site. Two cells closest to the targeted cell (white asterisks) share significant membrane contact with the irradiated cell. Between 5–69 minutes, the two astrocytes respond by extending their membranes toward the dead cell debris that they eventually surround and engulf. To better define the advancement of the responding astrocytes’ lamellae into the region of the irradiated cell, the boundary edge of the irradiated cell is traced with a solid black line in the post laser-irradiated region. This line is represented as a dashed black line in the subsequent high-magnification images. As the lamellae advances, vesicles form within the two responding cells starting 14 minutes following laser irradiation. The two other cells within the cluster, not in close proximity (and not sharing extensive membrane contact with the irradiated cell), do not exhibit any visible response following laser irradiation of the targeted cell.
answers: A1: The imaging modality is not mentioned in the caption.
A2: The caption does not specify a particular body region or anatomical area. It describes the procedure at the cellular level, particularly astrocytes within a cell cluster.

A4: The image shows irregularities in that it represents a laser-irradiated astrocyte exhibiting signs of cellular death.

A5: Yes, this image caption mentions various labels and indices that are significant. caption: Schematic results of the statistical parametric mapping (SPM) analysis of the contrasts.An uncorrected threshold of p = 0.001 was used for the voxel illustration. The SPM results were superimposed onto the SPM toolbox, xjView, with a template of brain structure indexed for the coordinate of the z axis according to the MNI space. Unimodal stimuli versus rests were indicated in yellow and red words for visual and auditory activation, respectively (FWE p< 0.05).Brain regions activated by visual and auditory stimuli, selected with FWE p value between 0.05 and 0.08, were in cyan and purple words, respectively, and were also depicted in yellow and red colors in the corresponding brain areas. A mask was formed to examine the bimodal task versus the average of unimodal tasks. The results shown in the green words and circles indicate three activated areas including the medial parietal lobe, BA9 and BA6, all outside the mask (FWE p value < 0.05). Finally, fifteen areas formed a ROI to examine the performance of subjects that outperformed bimodal tasks versus those who performed well in only one of the bimodal tasks. The ROI analysis shown in white circles revealed that the medial frontal lobe, FEF, BA6, BA7, and medial parietal lobe were negatively correlated with the contrast (t test, p < 0.05).

caption: Confocal microscopy to confirm the expression and determine the localization of HpdB. A plasmid-based translational fusion of HpdB was constructed in C. difficile strain 630Δerm, using the native hpdBCA promoter and hpdB coding sequence (without a stop codon) fused via a linker to the SNAP-tag to produce PhpdB-CDS-SNAP. The expression of HpdB linked to the SNAP-tag was confirmed by anti-SNAP western and mass spectrometry. Cultures of C. difficile 630Δerm and hpdC::CT, each carrying PhpdB-CDS-SNAP, were grown for 4 h in BHIS ±2 mg/ml p-HPA, then harvested in the presence of the SNAP-tag substrate TMR-Star before staining with Vectashield DAPI, and were imaged on a Zeiss LSM880 confocal microscope at 578-nm excitation and 603-nm emission (for TMR-Star) and 358-nm excitation and 463-nm emission for DAPI. The images are representative of three independent replicates.

caption: A 48-year-old man presented with initial painful calves followed by progressive painful arms and legs, shoulders, and knees. No hydrops or other clinical signs of arthritis. Urine sediment: albumin trace. Glomerular basal membrane antibody test negative. Viral serology negative. Chest X-ray and abdominal ultrasonography without abnormalities. Also 18F-FDG PET/CT showed pathological perisynovial uptake at the major joints, as well as pathological lumbar interspinous uptake in the soft tissue (bursae) lateral to both of the greater trochanters and dorsal to both of the tuber ischii. The diagnosis of PMR was made; after treatment with steroids, the patient became pain free, and the CRP values remained less than 10 mg/L [25].

caption: Scanning serial electron microscopy images analyzed for astrocytic endfeet width (highlighted in yellow). caption: Imaging examples in coronal and axial orientation. Patient B: a 55-year-old female patient with seronegative rheumatoid arthritis. There is severe synovitis in MCP V (arrowhead) and of the radiocarpal joint (arrow).
